title: StreamParser methods produce parallel ToolUseStart events for OpenAI partial tool_call deltas
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/streaming.rs:222-247
## Violation
parse_openai loops over delta.tool_calls and emits a ToolUseStart event whenever function.name is present. OpenAI streams tool_calls with name on the first delta only, then later deltas contain only arguments. The current code is correct for that — but it does NOT carry the OpenAI tool_call index, which is the field that tells the consumer which tool_use to attach the partial_json to. Without the index, a model that streams two parallel tool_calls (allowed by OpenAI's multi-tool API) cannot route arguments to the correct call. The Anthropic parser tracks block index explicitly (content_block_start carries an index); OpenAI parser drops it.
## Mandated refactor
Add a top-level index field to ToolUseStart and ToolUseInputDelta. Read tc.get("index") in parse_openai and include it. Document the consumer contract that partial_json is keyed by index. Provide a sanity assertion in ToolCallAccumulator that errors when two calls share an index without name resolution.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
